name: Suffragette
classification_notes: An all-caps roman capital design with medium contrast and slightly pronounced serifs, positioned as a display face for luxury branding rather than continuous text setting.
summary: An all-caps display face with medium contrast and restrained serifs, created for the Hermitage Hotel in Nashville and pitched at luxury packaging and couture branding.
description: Drawn originally for the Hermitage Hotel's Beaux-Arts identity, Suffragette blends classical roman capital proportions with a contemporary edge. The letterforms feature sans-serif-influenced medium contrast and subtly flared serifs, sitting somewhere between inscriptional and refined display. OpenType features include discretionary ligatures, an alternate R, multiple ampersand variants and case-sensitive forms, giving it more flexibility than a single-weight, single-style family might suggest.
foundry_blurb: Muccatypo describe Suffragette as an all-caps typeface introduced for the branding of the Hermitage Hotel in Nashville, inspired by the hotel's Beaux-Arts architecture with a 2020s sensibility. They position it as a unique roman capital design with sans serif-medium contrast shapes and slightly pronounced serifs, suited to luxury packaging and high-end couture for brands that want elegance with a playful edge.

name: Charlock
classification_notes: The glyph set and naming suggest a clean, contemporary geometric sans with variable weight axis; no serifs are present and the even stroke geometry points firmly to the geometric sans subcategory.
summary: A nine-weight geometric sans from Muccatypo, available as both static styles and a single variable font file with a full weight axis.
description: Charlock spans nine weights from Thin to Black with a matching variable font covering the same range. OpenType features include small capitals, small capitals from capitals, case-sensitive forms, swash alternates, proportional and tabular figures, and a stylistic set offering alternate forms for characters such as E, F, K, R, U, X and Y. Latin Extended-A coverage supports a wide range of European languages from Afrikaans to Zulu.
foundry_blurb: Muccatypo offer Charlock in ten styles: nine static weights from Thin to Black plus a variable font. The family includes OpenType features such as small capitals, swash variants, case-sensitive forms, and tabular and proportional figures, with broad Latin language support.

name: Seminario
classification_notes: Seminario is an all-caps display typeface with flared serifs and low contrast drawn from a 1912 Mexican magazine headline, placing it firmly in the Art Nouveau display tradition rather than a conventional serif or sans-serif category.
summary: An all-caps display typeface drawn from a 1912 headline in Mexico's La Revista de Revistas, with discreet flared serifs and an Art Nouveau sensibility.
description: Originally commissioned for the Latin American restaurant Curiosa by Jean-Georges at the Four Seasons Doha, Seminario takes its formal cues from early twentieth-century Mexican magazine typography. Its low contrast, feather-light strokes and carefully considered alternate sets — including floating capitals, underlined forms and roman capitals — make it best suited to large-scale, short-text applications. The family spans nine weights from Thin to Black, available as static cuts or as a single variable font.
foundry_blurb: Muccatypo describe Seminario as an all-caps typeface created for a Latin American restaurant in Doha, inspired by a 1912 headline from Mexico's national weekly La Revista de Revistas. Its Art Nouveau character comes through in discreet flared serifs, elegant ligatures and unusual alternate sets, designed to shine at large sizes and short lengths.

name: Fico Grotesque
classification_notes: A grotesque sans-serif with a variable axis spanning five widths from Condensed to Extended and five weights from Thin to Black, offering a broad range of static styles typical of contemporary grotesque superfamilies.
summary: A variable grotesque spanning five widths and five weights across 26 static styles, with OpenType features including stylistic sets, tabular figures and case-sensitive forms.
description: The family runs from Condensed to Extended across Thin, Light, Regular, Bold and Black, giving 25 static styles plus a variable font with continuous width and weight axes. OpenType support covers proportional and tabular figures, ordinals and three stylistic sets, making it adaptable for both display and text applications. The pharmaceutical-named specimen settings suggest a clinical or functional design sensibility.
foundry_blurb: Muccatypo offer Fico Grotesque as a 26-style family including a variable font, with width variants from Condensed through to Extended and weights from Thin to Black, supported by OpenType features such as stylistic sets, case-sensitive forms and tabular figures.

name: Mostly Serif
classification_notes: Mostly Serif sits at the border of legibility, with serif construction deliberately pushed to the edge of delirium; it is a display typeface first, with serif letterforms that challenge conventional letter recognition rather than aid reading.
summary: An experimental serif family born from an AIGA/NY anniversary poster commission, designed to sit on the edge of legibility by subverting conventional letterform recognition.
description: Originally conceived as a poster typeface for AIGA/NY's thirtieth anniversary, Mostly Serif plays with the question of what constitutes a letter, using serif construction to blur distinctions between characters and create deliberate ambiguity. Unusually, the Black weight is more legible than the Light, as heavier serifs and increased stroke thickness consolidate the forms rather than disperse them. The family is available in five static weights and as a variable font, supported across an extensive range of Latin-based languages.
foundry_blurb: Muccatypo describe Mostly Serif as a fully functioning font on the edge of legibility and delirium, created from a commission to design a poster for AIGA/NY's thirtieth anniversary. Given complete creative freedom, they set out to challenge what a letter is and how it is defined, designing letterforms that prompt the reader to question what they are seeing as they read. The Light version came first, with the Black following through heavier serifs and slightly increased stroke thickness, yielding the counterintuitive result that the darker weight reads more clearly.

name: NoExit Octagonal
classification_notes: The typeface takes its name and defining structural trait from its octagonal letterform geometry, a display-oriented construction with clipped corners that places it firmly in the geometric display category rather than any conventional grotesque or serif tradition.
summary: A variable octagonal display family spanning six width grades and four weights, with extensive OpenType alternates including multiple 'S' spine variants.
description: Built on octagonal letterform geometry with clipped corners, the family runs from Condensed through to UltraExpanded across four weights: Regular, SemiBold, Bold and Black. A variable font covers both the width and weight axes continuously, making it well suited to headline and branding work that demands flexibility across narrow and wide measures. OpenType features include case-sensitive forms, swash, fractions, single-storey 'a', double-storey 'g' and several distinct 'S' spine options, giving designers meaningful control over the character of the type.
foundry_blurb: Muccatypo offer NoExit Octagonal as a 25-style variable family with weight and width axes, covering six width grades from Condensed to UltraExpanded, each in four weights, alongside a comprehensive set of OpenType features including alternate letterforms and stylistic variants.

name: Sportivo Variable
classification_notes: Sportivo is a geometric sans with a variable slant axis, its upright and italic variants named after motion states, suggesting a clean, structured skeleton with expressive optical variation through obliquing.
summary: A variable sans from Muccatypo built around a slant axis, with five named instances running from a backward-leaning Reverse through a neutral Idling to a forward-leaning Forward.
description: The slant axis replaces the conventional italic with a spectrum of motion-inflected styles, labelled Reverse, SemiReverse, Idling, SemiForward and Forward, each corresponding to a distinct degree of obliquing. The family ships with an extensive ligature set, alternate letterforms including a two-storey 'a' and alternate 'U', plus circled numerals and pre-built fractions. Latin Extended coverage spans Central and Eastern European languages, making it serviceable across a wide range of Latin-script contexts.
foundry_blurb: Muccatypo offer Sportivo as both a variable font and a set of six static styles, with the variable version exposing a slant axis that moves through Reverse, SemiReverse, Idling, SemiForward and Forward positions. OpenType features include discretionary and standard ligatures, proportional figures, ordinals, fractions, alternate letterforms and superscripts.

name: NoExit Rounded
classification_notes: Rounded terminals on a geometric sans structure place this firmly in the rounded geometric category; the variable width and weight axes across condensed to ultra-expanded widths are characteristic of a contemporary geometric sans system.
summary: A rounded geometric sans with a variable font spanning seven widths from Condensed to UltraExpanded and four weights from Regular to Black, totalling 29 styles.
description: The family covers an unusually broad width range — Condensed through to UltraExpanded — each width available in Regular, SemiBold, Bold and Black, with a variable font consolidating the full system. OpenType features include single-storey and double-storey alternates for 'a' and 'g', swash, fractions and case-sensitive forms, giving flexibility for both display and text applications. The rounded terminals soften the geometric construction, making it well-suited to wayfinding, urban branding and signage contexts.
foundry_blurb: Muccatypo present NoExit Rounded as a 29-style variable family, offering weight and width axes that move continuously across seven width variants and four named weights, with a broad set of OpenType alternates for typographic control.
